The **Tallinn Marathon** is an annual road [marathon](/wiki/Marathon "Marathon"), held in [Tallinn](/wiki/Tallinn "Tallinn"), Estonia. It is held in September and is the biggest annual marathon in Estonia. Both the marathon and the half marathon held the day before are categorized as [Elite Label Road Races](/wiki/World_Athletics_Label_Road_Races "World Athletics Label Road Races") by [World Athletics](/wiki/World_Athletics "World Athletics").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tallinn_Marathon&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The first regularly-held marathon was held in 2010.[[3]](#cite_note-about-3) Prior to this, the marathon was first held in 1989 with about 100 runners, and a half marathon and a 10 km race had been held regularly since 2000.[[3]](#cite_note-about-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In 2015, Estonian Marti Medar set a world record for completing a marathon while dribbling a basketball, finishing in 3:04:15.[[5]](#cite_note-2017.basketball-5) In 2017, Medar set a world record for completing a marathon while dribbling two basketballs at the same time, doing so in 3:54:16, and then set it again in 2021 with a time of 3:36:36.[[5]](#cite_note-2017.basketball-5)[[1]](#cite_note-2021.basketball-1)

The 2020 in-person edition of the race was cancelled due to the [coronavirus pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic"), with all registrants given the option of running the race virtually, transferring their entry to 2021, or obtaining a refund.[[6]](#cite_note-2020.cancellation-6)[[7]](#cite_note-2020.cancellation.options-7)
